prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='catboost', seasonality_champion='CWT_catboost', recommended_method='inverse_rmse_weights'. Narrative: catboost excels at trend tracking with lowest slope error (0.565), while CWT_catboost captures seasonality best (0.2384). NaiveMovingAverage dominates overall RMSE but tier1 models show substantial seasonal representation differences (variance=0.5471). Early horizons favor wavelet-based models, late horizons favor tree ensembles. Given high seasonality variance and moderate rmse spread, inverse_rmse_weights with seasonality basis is recommended to balance diverse model behaviors.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "CWT_svr", "late": "rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "CWT_svr", "THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "ARIMA", "FT_rf", "catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "inverse_rmse_weights",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "seasonality",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.2471,
    "high_disagreement": false,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "catboost excels at trend tracking with lowest slope error (0.565), while CWT_catboost captures seasonality best (0.2384). NaiveMovingAverage dominates overall RMSE but tier1 models show substantial seasonal representation differences (variance=0.5471). Early horizons favor wavelet-based models, late horizons favor tree ensembles. Given high seasonality variance and moderate rmse spread, inverse_rmse_weights with seasonality basis is recommended to balance diverse model behaviors."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "dba_combination"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35": {"top_k": 5, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "robust_smape",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity per horizon, favoring best_per_horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.288 ≥ 0.25 suggests robust methods like dba_combination and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. n_windows=3 is very few, so small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.35 prevent overfitting. pattern_analyst_insights recommends 'inverse_rmse_weights' as the method hint, and high_seasonality_variance=true supports weighted approaches that balance diverse model behaviors."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - baselines never leak, and weighted/selection candidates use anti-leakage expanding window (train_window=5) per eval_config. Universe leaderboards show robust_median performs better across all metrics but diversity rules don't require adding it since we already have both selection and weighted types. No redundancy issues (max 2 of any type < 4 threshold).",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: catboost excels at trend tracking with lowest slope error (0.565), while CWT_catboost captures seasonality best (0.2384). NaiveMovingAverage dominates overall RMSE but tier1 models show substantial seasonal representation differences (variance=0.5471). Early horizons favor wavelet-based models, late horizons favor tree ensembles. Given high seasonality variance and moderate rmse spread, inverse_rmse_weights with seasonality basis is recommended to balance diverse model behaviors.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity per horizon, favoring best_per_horizon selection. relative_spread_mean=0.288 ≥ 0.25 suggests robust methods like dba_combination and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. n_windows=3 is very few, so small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.35 prevent overfitting. pattern_analyst_insights recommends 'inverse_rmse_weights' as the method hint, and high_seasonality_variance=true supports weighted approaches that balance diverse model behaviors.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has 4 candidates with diverse types: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - baselines never leak, and weighted/selection candidates use anti-leakage expanding window (train_window=5) per eval_config. Universe leaderboards show robust_median performs better across all metrics but diversity rules don't require adding it since we already have both selection and weighted types. No redundancy issues (max 2 of any type < 4 threshold).
